content.problemStatement: Write a program that allows the user to insert integer number, then check If a number is even or odd
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing "Even" if N is even, otherwise output "Odd".

sampleTestCases.explanation: 8 is divisible by 2, so the program prints "Even".
1 leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 2, so the program prints "Odd".
10 is even and 11 is odd, producing the two lines accordingly.

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
Odd
Even
Odd
Even
Odd
Even

solution.approach: For each integer N we only need to know whether it is divisible by 2. The remainder operator (N % 2) yields 0 for even numbers and 1 for odd numbers. Iterate over the T test cases, compute N % 2 and print the corresponding word. This is optimal because the answer is obtained in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T numbers.

code.Python3:
import sys

data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T = int(data[0])
out = []
idx = 1
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(data[idx])
    idx += 1
    out.append('Even' if N % 2 == 0 else 'Odd')
sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
